title: Groundwork Atomic Design Artifact
description: Schema for a single atomic design-artifact file's YAML frontmatter. One artifact per Markdown file under .sdlc/design/<components|interfaces>/<ID>-<kebab-title>.md. A shared base plus a per-type branch: component specs (CMP-) carry responsibility/boundary/depends_on; interface specs (IF-) carry provider/operations/interaction/error_modes. The dependency edge lives once as CMP.depends_on -> IF.provider (there is no consumers field). Branch fields are declared inside their type's `then` clause, never at top level, so unevaluatedProperties:false rejects a component field on an interface (and vice versa) while still composing through the branch.

id.description: Categorical, zero-padded, stable ID. Prefix encodes type: CMP->component, IF->interface. An optional uppercase category infix is allowed (e.g. CMP-AUTH-004).

title.description: Short human-readable title (matches the kebab-case used in the filename).

description.description: One clear statement of what this element is.

traces_from.description: Upstream traceability: requirement IDs (FR-/NFR-/CON-/BR-/UC-) this element satisfies. May be empty. Existence is resolved by the cross-artifact validator (STO-102), not this schema.

traces_to.description: Downstream traceability to ADRs, diagrams, code, and tests. Any key may be omitted or empty.

status.description: Lifecycle status. IDs are never re-used; set status to obsolete instead of deleting.

scope.description: RESERVED (agile planning scope). Present-but-optional; mirrors the requirement schema.

parent_scope.description: RESERVED. Parent scope ID, or null. Present-but-optional.

allOf.$comment: Component branch. Declaring responsibility/boundary/depends_on here (not at top level) is what makes them valid ONLY on a component: on an interface this `then` never applies, the fields stay unevaluated, and unevaluatedProperties:false rejects them.

responsibility.description: The single clear purpose of the unit.

boundary.description: Whether the unit is inside the system boundary or an external system modelled as a component (C4).

depends_on.description: The interface contracts this component consumes. IF- IDs only; may be empty. Existence is resolved by the design validator.

allOf.$comment: Interface branch. Same mechanism in reverse: provider/operations/interaction/error_modes are valid only on an interface.

provider.description: The single component that provides this contract. Exactly one CMP- ID.

operations.description: The operations this contract exposes, at architecture altitude (name + summary; no payload schemas).

error_modes.description: How this contract can fail. An interface that does not say how it fails is a gap; at least one mode is required.

$comment: Diagnostic note: when a branch field is present but INVALID (e.g. a bad provider pattern), its `then` applicator fails, contributes no evaluated-property annotations, and unevaluatedProperties then also reports the branch fields as unexpected. The precise per-field error is still emitted alongside the noisier line; this is an accepted tradeoff of the base-plus-branch composition (design spec C.4).
